review_text: Good
Great place to stay 🙂
first time stayed here for 5 days, should have stayed 10 days, beautiful spot, absolute spotless amenities, friendly staff, great sites, close to town (5 minutes) its got the lot.
What a magnificent place. Great for kids and so much room. Fantastic beach and shops 👌

Went here to stay for a business trip, camping in a tent and all, we stay on the foreshore part of the ground where the beach is literally 10 steps away

WOWIE what a charming, beautiful, STUNNING place, we even did the non powered site as we only stayed a night

I don't think Inverloch is known for its coffee, BUT and thats a huge BUT everything else more than made up for it

Loved it so much, the following g days I have bought nearly all camping gear to bring the family, this time on a powered site

AND

Your can bring furry children (only in the colder seasons)

See you soon Inverloch🫶✌️🥰
30 Secs walk from the carpark. Salmon here during season. Nothing better..

Whilst we found the park to be in a great position and the staff friendly there were a couple of things that marred the experience. We stayed on site 169 which was very undulating both front to back and side to side. Impossible to get our 21 ft van level even though it was a large site. The sullage pit was right beside where our awning sat. The speed of some drivers in the park left a lot to be desired and made it unsafe for kids. More signage is required and maybe education at check in!

We have travelled to inverloch many times and this was our third stay at the Ingenia Foreshore Campground. The position is great, the beach here is good for young  children and,  off season,  it feels spacious. The down side is the ammenities, they are in very poor repair. General maintenance has not been addressed, broken tiles, dripping taps, broken hinges and handles, corrode fittings, cracked and lifted laminate. In addition the facilities are not at all clean with scum and mould overtaking! It’s disappointing that ingenia cannot allocate the resources to give the foreshore campground the refresh it deserves.

Was a nice spot to camp however everyone around us were playing music on speakers super loud until 2 in the morning. The next night a man who I assume was a worker on patrol (he never gave us his title or anything) came at 10pm on a Saturday saying that “this was our second warning and when he gives us a third we won’t be happy” we were sitting around having one drink before bed and chatting. We’d never received a warning that night in the first place and we weren’t being loud to warrant his aggressive behaviour. We told him we’d never spoke to him before but if he wanted us to tone it down it wasn’t an issue he then proceeded to keep accusing us.
One of our go to parks on the foreshore.  Best beach for kids and paddleboarding.  Awesome seeing the massive stingrays at the pier

We booked here because we were told it’s owned by same company and we can use the pool and the water splash as the other big4 one and since the main Big4 one had no availability so we booked here. We were told it was “a very short walk” from the sites, but on arrival our group was given the farthest possible sites. To reach the pools, we ended up walking around 20 minutes each way. Could have been communicated better to avoid this miscommunication
Great spot, CV lose to the beach and good sites. Had an awesome weekend

Location Location Location

Laid back and located right on the dunes of Anderson Inlet. You are literally steps from the water. The park is a flat 10-15 minute walk into the Inverloch township for cafes and the supermarket.

The amenities blocks are modern and very clean.
The reception team is super friendly and accommodating with early check-ins if the site is ready.

One issue on the foreshore is that the sites are tight and the borders are not well marked leading to disputes with "neighbours" upon arrival. I would recommend that the borders b/w sites on the forshore are clearly marked. Wose thing starting your break is to argue with your neighbour that they need to move their van 2 meters over after they have set up.

Otherwise great and will keep coming back.
Awesome customer service, clean amenities and most of the site are grassy, maybe another amenities block would help.

I did a great review for this place last time and here I am again! Just spoke to Chelsea on front desk (over phone) and was just so helpful- truly! Really appreciate your customer service. Have a great day!
Great value for money caravan park in outstanding location with very modern and clean amenities
Off season so plenty of options as parked caravan Only mnetres to inlet beach
Very close to the ocean. This place is not very commercial. It’s more in a natural environment.
A great park! This was our first holiday in our van and it was such a great experience. The park was welcoming, the facilities were great. Its a great place to stay.

We stayed for a night, and really enjoyed it.
The staff were super friendly and accommodating. We requested to move sites to be closer to the beach access and it was done without any issues. We would highly recommend and definitely come back ☺️

Didn't like it was a public road anyone can drive in so not heap safe for kids. Not all toilets block was working only the middle one. Which was not good having a little one. The only thing going for it was right on top of the beach
Great clean place with plenty to do
Great location, close to amenities and shops watch out for thieves
Good for chill with family and friends
Great place to stay, the staff could not have been more helpful, both when we booked over the phone and when we got here and wanted to change sites. They went out of their way to please us.👍
Great camping area, gets a little windy at times though.  Facilities are good.

This was our first time visiting Inveloch and we loved it. We have a large caravan and we’re pleasantly surprised to have been allocated a very spacious site (#103), #102 would have also been plenty of room if you also want a spacious site.
Beach is only meters away and the shrubbery between the beach and site served as a great windbreaker.
Amenities were cleaned thoroughly each morning and there are many throughout the caravan park. We were there during the busy Christmas/New year season and found the park had a really great vibe, family friendly and very quiet at night.
Great walking tracks and the esplanade was great for bike riding.

A large linear park that is well maintained.
Some nice powered and unpowered sites, with beach access from many gaps in the tree line.
They do tend to squeeze a lot of vehicles into the areas though.
Lovely staff, clean amenities and right by the inlet.
Super good location, close to Melbourne and walking distance to Inverloch shopping centre.

Beautiful foreshore experience here at Inverloch. Loved that it was separated from the Big 4 next door. Felt more spacious with access to the beach. (Wish we had nicer weather though)
Great coffee and short walk to the Inverloch  cafe and cute little Beer Garden at the Sandbar. Didn't feel the need to head into town except for groceries.
Amenities were clean and grass was well kept. Only thing I wish they did was mark out the blocks/sites on busy weekends.
Other than that, lovely walking paths for those small treks and an overall great place to stay. Was great having an easy check-in experience.

Booked for Melb cup weekend on early bird booking, thinking it’s the right thing to do for us and the park. Nope had to cancel called up and told there is no refund on early bird bookings. So don’t take full payment on early bird bookings, maybe just take a deposit instead of rorting people out of their money. Will never book with big four or Ingenia parks again. This is something that was relevant during the Covid times not now. And l will not renew the useless Big4 membership again. It is written in the terms and conditions is a very poor customer relations cop out. Very poor

Falsely advertises having a camp kitchen on the website (see attached). We called to find out where it was and was abruptly told there wasn't any camp kitchen. Staff were flat and not friendly. Location is the main positive for this campsite, right on the beach.
Convenient location. Clean amenities
Absolutely loving it here.  Just what we had hoped for.  And so close to the beach.  Amenities are spotless.  Would def come back on any future trips.
Great place to stay, right on the inlet, great facilities
Good, but some issues with the amenities.
Nice place for camping. Clean facility. Sadly the weather was not great when we visit. Will com again.
Great couple of nights stay
